reasoning: Two topologies are "not comparable" iff neither is contained in the other. The Lean statement says ¬(every Rl-open set is K_topology-open) ∧ ¬(every K_topology-open set is Rl-open), which is exactly the negation of both inclusions and hence precisely "not comparable". The definition of Rl uses `Ico a b = {x | a ≤ x < b}` as basis sets, matching the standard lower limit topology [a,b). The set K = {1/(n+1) : n ∈ ℕ} = {1, 1/2, 1/3, ...} matches Munkres' K = {1/n : n ∈ ℤ⁺}, and K_topology is generated by Ioo a b together with (Ioo a b) \ K, matching the standard K-topology basis. The encoding of "not comparable" as the conjunction of two negations precisely captures the informal claim.
